headline: GameStop’s eBay Stake Puts Fresh Attention On Valuation And Governance
summary: GameStop, led by CEO Ryan Cohen, recently made an unsolicited multi billion dollar takeover offer for NasdaqGS:EBAY that eBay's board rejected as not credible. Following the rejected bid, GameStop increased its economic exposure to eBay through derivatives and public filings, now holding more than 6% economic interest. Cohen is publicly pushing for leadership changes and cost cuts at eBay, signaling potential for further board level confrontations. eBay, listed as NasdaqGS:EBAY, operates a...

headline: GameStop boosts eBay stake to 6.6% amid takeover push
summary: Investing.com -- GameStop has increased its stake in eBay to approximately 6.6% from around 5%, according to a regulatory filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday.

headline: GameStop increases eBay stake to 6.5%
summary: GameStop (GME) has increased its stake in eBay (EBAY) by over 6.5% after the e-commerce platform rejected GameStop's $56 billion takeover bid earlier this month. Market Catalysts Anchor Julie Hyman uses AlphaSpace — Yahoo Finance's new investing platform — to take a closer look at GameStop and eBay's respective stock performances amid these headlines. Earlier this week, Yahoo Finance launched AlphaSpace, a premium investment platform that combines data, charting, news, analysis, and more to help retail investors understand markets and make smart decisions faster.

headline: GameStop increases stake in eBay to more than 6%
summary: GameStop increased its stake in eBay to about 6.6% from ‌around 5%, according to a regulatory filing, the latest step ‌in the videogame retailer's push to buy the e-commerce giant.  "Together, the 25,000 shares ​of Common Stock beneficially owned directly and the shares of Common Stock underlying Put/Call Pairs constitute approximately 6.55% of the outstanding shares of Common Stock," the filing made by GameStop with the U.S. Securities and ‌Exchange Commission said.  Earlier this ⁠month GameStop Chief Executive Officer Ryan Cohen surprised Wall Street with an unsolicited offer to buy eBay ⁠for roughly $56 billion.

"Together, the 25,000 shares ​of Common Stock beneficially owned directly and the shares of Common Stock underlying Put/Call Pairs constitute approximately 6.55% of the outstanding shares of Common Stock," the filing made by GameStop with the U.S. Securities and ‌Exchange Commission said.  Earlier this ⁠month GameStop Chief Executive Officer Ryan Cohen surprised Wall Street with an unsolicited offer to buy eBay ⁠for roughly $56 billion.
